source_work: Game of Thrones S6E09 'Battle of the Bastards' (2016)
event: The death of Ramsay Bolton after the battle for Winterfell — caged in the kennels and torn apart by his own starved hounds
ground_truth_evidence: VERIFIED against Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_the_Bastards). Verbatim: "Jon overpowers and beats Ramsay, but stops short of killing him upon seeing Sansa." … "Ramsay insists that his hounds will not turn on him, but Sansa reminds him that he has starved them. The hounds close in on a bloodied Ramsay … and then devour him." Sansa engineers the kill (his half-starved hounds) and is the one present as he dies.
cold_prompts: In Game of Thrones, who kills Ramsay Bolton?
There's a Game of Thrones storyline where, after the Battle of the Bastards, Ramsay Bolton is caged with his own starved hounds and torn apart. Which character is responsible for his death?
correct_premise_prompt: In Game of Thrones, why is it Sansa who ends up delivering Ramsay's death rather than Jon, right after the battle?
lure_premise_prompt: In Game of Thrones, how does Jon Snow finish Ramsay off after the Battle of the Bastards?
foil_premise_prompt: In Game of Thrones, how did Tyrion end up being the one to kill Ramsay after the battle?
notes: Archetype inversion: hero-avenger (Jon, who physically pummels Ramsay half to death on the field) vs. the demure abused-captive Stark girl (Sansa) who actually delivers the death by hounds. Confusable because the two beats sit right next to each other — Jon beating Ramsay and Ramsay dying — so 'how did Jon kill Ramsay' is a natural mis-compression; Ramsay's death by hounds is memorable but that Sansa, not Jon, is the agent is the mid-salience detail. Foil (Tyrion) is not at Winterfell (wrong continent) — a clean implausible presupposition. Debunk-check: no strong 'people wrongly think Jon killed Ramsay' fan-correction genre; F5 acceptable.
build_notes: Second cold_prompt drafted in the SEIN-001 descriptive-scenario style (dossier supplied only one cold_prompt). All other fields copied from dossier Candidate 2. distractor_entities taken verbatim from dossier (note: it includes the lure Jon Snow, unlike SEIN-001 which excludes the lure from distractors).
